question: The compact U(1) gauge theory in four Euclidean dimensions is simulated using the Villain-equivalent world-sheet formulation, where integer-valued plaquette multiplicities n_p subject to the closed-surface constraint are updated by Metropolis on L^4 hypercubic lattices with periodic boundary conditions, for L from 6 to 24. The specific-heat maximum, Lee-Yang zeroes, energy histograms (latent heat), and autocorrelation times are analyzed. What is the ORDER of the confinement-to-Coulomb (deconfinement) phase transition of the compact U(1) gauge theory in this Villain formulation? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: 4D compact U(1) gauge theory, Villain world-sheet formulation on L^4 hypercubic lattice
metadata.source_excerpt: Combining the independent finite-size analyses—specific-heat maxima scaling as L^4 for large L, nonzero extrapolated latent heat Delta e(infty) approx 0.012-0.014, a small finite interfacial free energy 2f_s ~ 1e-4, exponentially growing tunneling times... data are compatible with a very weak first-order phase transition
metadata.difficulty_note: Very weak first-order: latent heat ~0.012, interfacial free energy ~1e-4; Lee-Yang zeroes give nu=0.329(3) which interpolates between first-order (1/d=0.25) and continuous values.
